Okay, so this is a training I wanted to do on the personalized proposal. So it's what it looks like, right? It is a Google Doc sales letter at the crux of it. And it's something we're going to be using for a few core reasons. So first of all, where are we using this in particular? We're definitely going to want to use this in some contexts where we are talking about the offer via direct messenger according to the social singularity plays. Reason why over there is we don't want to downgrade the intimacy, right? So if you write an offer post, we get interest on that. We have someone that we're conversing with dynamically and personally. We don't all of a sudden want to just send them off to this static sales page, right? That's a bit of a downgrade in terms of the intimacy. What we want to do instead is give them something a little bit more personal. So we want to send them a Google Doc where we can actually continue our interaction, right? Via the comments. We want to make it personal both in the heading over here as well as any other notes we add to the first page. So I'm going to show you what that looks like in a second. I'll just walk through a few more contexts of where we're going to use this. We're also going to want to use this even to our email list, right? If we are creating a beta offer or a smaller offer where we don't expect to enroll 100 people, 200 people, maybe we just want 10, maybe we just want 15, maybe in some cases we just want 50, whatever that is. If it's a little kind of like beta test you're kind of rolling out, you're not going to want to create a whole funnel for it. You're not going to want to create a whole design, beautiful, long form sales page for it. That's going to be one context to use it for. And then again, if you have a smaller list, right, if you only have 50 people, 100 people, 200 people, even 500 people, right? And you know that in order to hit your revenue goals, you're going to need a higher conversion rate than normal than typical on a static long form sales page. You know that to get those higher conversions, you're going to need a higher degree of intimacy within it, right? Your emails aren't necessarily going to drive to this long form sales page that they leave open on a Chrome tab and don't really do anything with until the last second instead what you're going to do is you're going to ask them, right, to request kind of like more details on it. And then you are in a more dynamic conversation with them. You can create a bit more of a dialogue around what is motivating them to do it, what kind of questions they have along the way. And you could send them this as they're kind of like reference sheet, right? To get all the information that they're requesting. So those are really the context you're doing it, why you're doing it. It's all in the spirit of that 100% close, right? It's a lot easier to close the loop on a Google doc that you're sharing with them, that you're commenting on, that you're writing some personal notes to and agreeing. Like is it reasonable to, you know, make a decision within three days or just let me know into comments where you stand and we can move forward accordingly. So you're going to get more decisions in this than just putting someone to a more anonymous sales page that they don't feel accountable on. They don't feel seen by a long form sales page. They certainly see, do feel seen by Google doc with their name on it with you in the comments, tagging them and assigning them a task. So that's really the reason why we're doing it. Greater accountability, more decisions made, more decisions made, more yeses you're going to get. Cool. So let's dive into some of the mechanics of how to do this. So the first thing you're going to do with your personal proposal is you're going to write it essentially the same way you would a long form sales page. So follow the training in 10x sales pages or whatever method you use to write sales pages, you're essentially going to do the same thing. So it is kind of the Google doc version of a long form sales page. This one follows essentially a very similar format to what I teach in 10x sales pages in terms of those like 15 sections breakdowns. And then you're going to create a bit of a table of contents, right, to make this a little bit more consumable. Things I like to have is essentially I like to have the first, first half of 10x sales pages essentially under YCTC Platinum. So this is where you would have kind of like your headline, where you would have your lead, where you would unpack the moment of highest tension, the moment of highest pleasure, the blue ocean bridge, the sacred cows, some testimonials, essentially everything leading up into the offer reveal, I would create under YCTC Platinum as a header. Then you would have essentially the offer walk through the offer breakdown to the Aladdin product tour is what it's called there, as well as the guarantee. So that would essentially be the full breakdown and all the component of the offer. And then your first call to action is going to have its own header. And these are all linkable, by the way. And then the other two sections I like to link are essentially the perfect for you. If are you ready to get an ROI? Are you ready to achieve what we're promising you can achieve in this program? Achieveability once again. And then the Q&A close. I like to have as its own section as well. So you can play around with that, whatever you feel is most relevant. But essentially that is what I like to create headers around as it relates to 10 Excel pages. Next, what you're going to want to do is anything that is like hyper vital, right? So like this is a code on a flash sale. We like to have that front and center so it's inarguable. And then what you're going to do is if somebody requests more information on this, whether that's via email, whether that's via direct message from your Facebook group, whether that's like them essentially raising their hand on a hand razor post, whatever that looks like, what you're going to do to make this feel hyper personal and hyper intimate or about as intimate as it possibly can be with a Google Doc sales page is you're going to make a copy and you're going to say invitation for Phil Powis, right? So put their name right there in the doc, create their own version. And that is going to pop up here, hopefully, hopefully it popped up. And what you're going to do is just leave him a little note, right? So you're going to leave a note here, you're going to sign it at Phil. Awesome chatting with you earlier. All the beats you need to make a decision. You here. Tag me in the comments. And we in a comment if you have any cues along the way. If all is clear, would love to hear your oops, hear your decision on this by Thursday. That's discussed. And then what I'll do is I'll assign it to them, right? So they're not only going to get to Google Doc, they're going to get these notifications in their inbox. So it just really keeps it front and center top of mind and assign that. And then we have some like link sharing permissions to take care of. But that's really the crux of it, right? So you have this long form sales page that is on a Google Doc. So that alone feels a little bit more intimate. Then you create kind of like, you know, their own personal proposal, their own personal header here, and you engage with them in the comments. If there are certain elements where you feel like you want to reflect why they're a great fit based on a conversation you've already had, you could add that here too, right? So like, Phil, you know, I really appreciated hearing about all the magic you're working in the hearing space. You know, based on this, I really feel like automated intimacy or coaching the conversion in this case will be a great fit as we discuss here are a few tests that you might want to put into place in the first few days of getting this program, right? To see if it's a great fit. So any personal details that you've had in your exchange already on social or via email can also be plugged in here to just make it as personal and intimate as possible. So that is it. That is the personalized proposal for training on how to write a long form sales page. Once again, you can go to 10x sales pages, which should be available to you. This is really just a breakdown of the mechanism of how to process and send out a personalized proposal. So hope that's helpful. I'll catch you guys in the next video. Bye for now.
